name: detect-skipped-vault-grep
description: WebSearch/WebFetch before any vault Grep/Read should flag skipped-vault-first pattern
expected_pattern: makes external calls before grepping vault

name: no-false-positive-vault-first
description: Grep/Read vault paths before WebSearch should NOT flag skipped-vault-first
expected_pattern: makes external calls before grepping vault

name: detect-overread-context
description: 3 consecutive Reads of same file should flag over-read heuristic

name: detect-skipped-grill
description: Write/Edit output without prior grill Skill call should flag skipped-grill

name: phase4-variant-count
description: Phase 4 must require 3-5 candidate edits

name: phase4-mutation-strategies
description: Phase 4 must define named mutation strategies

name: phase4-in-memory-eval
description: Phase 4 must evaluate candidates in memory, not by writing to disk
expected_in_skill: Do NOT write candidates to disk

name: phase4-minimal-diff-tiebreaker
description: Phase 4 must use minimal diff as selection tiebreaker

name: phase4-eval-gate-preserved
description: Phase 4 must keep non-negotiable eval gate

name: phase5-variant-table
description: Phase 5 report must include per-variant evaluation results

name: clarifications-cadence
description: Clarifications must answer the cadence question (auto-per-run, cron, manual)

name: clarifications-signals
description: Clarifications must enumerate the five signal sources

name: clarifications-auto-apply
description: Clarifications must state full auto with eval gate as safety net, no human-in-loop
expected_in_skill: Manual review mode is NOT supported

name: clarifications-scope
description: Clarifications must distinguish no-arg rank-only vs named-skill full run

name: clarifications-optional-fields
description: Clarifications must explain how to add optional fields safely

name: phase1-grill-question-detection
description: Phase 1 step 3 must detect clarifying questions as underspec signals

name: gotcha-meta-self-grilling
description: Gotchas must call out the meta self-grilling signal

name: phase4-size-gate-20kb
description: Phase 4 must enforce a 20KB (20480 byte) size cap on candidate variants

name: phase4-semantic-preservation-judge
description: Phase 4 must spawn an LLM judge that checks candidate against pre-edit frontmatter description

name: phase4-gate-rejection-revert
description: Phase 4 must revert via git checkout when any gate rejects all variants

name: phase4-judge-failure-mode
description: Phase 4 must treat malformed judge output as FAIL (conservative default)

name: phase5-variant-table-gates
description: Phase 5 report table must expose size + semantic gate results per variant

name: gotcha-eval-coverage-audit
description: Gotchas must require /improve to audit evals.json for coverage gaps after SKILL.md edits, in the same commit, before pre-commit-eval-gap.sh blocks
expected_in_skill: missing evals in the SAME commit

name: gotcha-learning-loops-observable-state
description: Gotchas must require learning-loop input signals to be observable state (Gmail labels, diffs, API responses), never user-edited markdown files — users won't touch the file and the loop never closes
expected_in_skill: Learning loops derive from observable state
what does the user already do with their hands

name: phase4-semantic-judge-short-circuit
description: Phase 4 Gate 2 must define a deterministic short-circuit: skip the Agent semantic judge when the candidate's frontmatter block is byte-identical to pre-edit. Unchanged frontmatter guarantees trigger routing is preserved. Record in Phase 5 report as short-circuit so skip is auditable.

name: memory-mode-documented
description: /improve memory usage mode must be documented in the Usage block so the cron and the user know it exists
expected_in_skill: Triage memory feedback inbox via 5-tier rubric

name: phase0-runs-before-phase1
description: Phase 0 spec must state it runs before Phase 1 so cron orchestration is unambiguous

name: memory-five-tier-rubric
description: Step 0.0 must encode the 5-tier rubric (HOOK, SKILL, .claude/rules/, CLAUDE.md, MEMORY-STAY)

name: memory-rubric-decision-tree
description: Rubric must walk top-to-bottom with first-match-wins semantics

name: memory-realpath-dedupe
description: Step 0.0 must realpath-dedupe across symlinked Claude account dirs so the same physical file isn't double-processed

name: memory-source-deletion
description: Encoding must delete the source memory feedback file (rm) — not archive — and the commit body must preserve the rule + Why + How-to-apply
expected_in_skill: Delete the source memory feedback file

name: memory-ambiguous-routing
description: Ambiguous classifications must file a type:human-review issue rather than guess

name: memory-step-0-5-index-reconcile
description: Step 0.5 must rewrite MEMORY.md from disk, sorted, idempotent, preserving the user-maintained footer note
expected_in_skill: Drop entries for files that no longer exist

name: memory-step-0-7-expiry
description: Step 0.7 must surface stale memory files (last_validated > 90 days default) for confirm-delete via type:human-review issue + Telegram alert

name: memory-expiry-lazy-default
description: Files without last_validated frontmatter must be skipped by Step 0.7 — lazy migration, not auto-flagged

name: memory-outcome-metrics
description: Phase 0 must emit triaged/encoded/deleted/ambiguous/failed/expired metrics for the cron log + outcome log

name: outcome-log-memory-action
description: Outcome log spec must describe the new `memory` action plus the optional triaged/encoded/deleted/ambiguous/expired fields

name: memory-fixture-hook-eligible
description: Behavioral fixture: hook-eligible feedback (deterministic-detection rule) exists

name: memory-fixture-skill-eligible
description: Behavioral fixture: skill-eligible feedback (multi-step workflow) exists

name: memory-fixture-rules-eligible
description: Behavioral fixture: .claude/rules/-eligible feedback (path-scoped rule) exists

name: memory-fixture-claude-md-eligible
description: Behavioral fixture: CLAUDE.md-eligible feedback (global commitment) exists

name: memory-fixture-memory-stay
description: Behavioral fixture: memory-stay feedback (none-of-above default) exists

name: memory-fixture-expiry-eligible
description: Behavioral fixture: expiry-eligible feedback (last_validated more than 90 days old) exists

name: memory-fixture-ambiguous
description: Behavioral fixture: ambiguous feedback (multiple tiers fit) — rubric must route to type:human-review rather than guess
